Global (All).summary: Tensions rise between US and Iran after drone attack on US base in Syria.
Russia and Ukraine trade accusations of shelling along conflict zone.
European powers urge Iran to respect nuclear deal amid concerns over enrichment activities.

United States.title: Election Interference Fears Amid FBI Probe Into 2024 Election-Related Data Breach
United States.summary: The US Justice Department has opened an investigation into a data breach that may have compromised election information ahead of the 2024 US presidential election, sparking fears of potential interference.

United States.title: Protests Erupt as Supreme Court Considers to Allow States to Ban Abortions After 20 Weeks
United States.summary: Protests have been erupting across the United States as the Supreme Court weighs a proposal to permit states to ban abortions after 20 weeks.

United States.title: Mass Shooting Occurs in California Leaving Multiple Injured
United States.summary: A mass shooting occurred in California, leaving multiple people injured and sparking concerns about gun violence in the US.

South Korea.title: Seoul Mayor Election Dispute
South Korea.summary: South Korea's top election body on Monday ordered a by-election for Seoul mayor, upholding a lower court's ruling that ousted the current mayor over a vote-rigging scandal.

South Korea.title: Moon Jae-in Impeachment Call grows
South Korea.summary: Opposition lawmakers in South Korea's parliament have submitted an impeachment request for President Yoon Suk Yeol amid corruption allegations, but it is uncertain if enough lawmakers will back it.

South Korea.title: North Korean Missile Launch Sparks Fears
South Korea.summary: A North Korean ballistic missile launched into the sea sparked regional security concerns. South Korean authorities say the launch does not pose a direct threat, however the act demonstrates Pyongyang's ability to launch a missile within striking distance of Tokyo and Seoul.

Japan.title: Typhoon Hinnamor lands in Japan, forcing evacuation
Japan.summary: A powerful typhoon made landfall in western Japan on July 10, 2024, with the Meteorological Agency warning of strong winds and heavy rain. Several evacuation orders were issued. The storm was the second to hit the country in a week, causing disruption and concern. The risk level remains high, especially in areas prone to flooding and landslides.

Japan.title: Nuclear Reactor Leaks Water into Japanese Sea
Japan.summary: A leak at a nuclear reactor in Japan has spilled water into the sea, sparking concerns over radioactive contamination. The reactor, operated by Tokyo Electric Power, is in a state of cold shutdown, and the leak is believed to have happened during a maintenance procedure.

Japan.title: Japan's PM announces plans to raise consumption tax rate
Japan.summary: In a bid to stimulate economic growth, Japan's Prime Minister has proposed increasing the consumption tax rate from 10% to 12%. The move aims to plug the budget gap and fund social welfare programs. However, it may also lead to higher living costs and impact consumer spending.
